desc:
The dark water surrounds you with a chilling touch, reaching to your very bones
and quickly exhausting your strength. A layer of sediment and rubble covers the
bottom, making the ground look fairly level. No algae or other plants grow in
the dark water or on the surface which seems to be only a few feet above.
name: Underwater

desc:
Beneath the lake is a floor of sharp rocks making diving a hazardous adventure.
With every swim stroke care has to be taken not to collide with the rocks that
are well-hidden in the dark water. The floor slopes gently downwards to the east
and the south, and there is a faint stir in the water as if something was moving
in its depths.
name: Underwater

desc:
Beneath the surface of the dark underground lake the water is chillingly cold. A
springy bed of mud obscures the primordial rock that forms the bottom of the
lake. To the south is an opening into a dark water-filled cavern. The surface of
the water is only a few feet above.
name: Underwater

desc:
At the bottom of the lake the dark water is freezing cold. A bed of mud and
rubble covers the bottom which is fairly level except for a few rocks peering up
through the mud. Occasional stirrings in the water indicate that something is
moving in the lake. The surface is only a few feet above.
name: Underwater

desc:
Barely visible through the dark water, the cavern walls bend to form a curving
ceiling of hard rock. A hole in the flat floor near the southern wall leads into
a dark pit, only accessible to the most skilled swimmer. To the north is an
opening that leads into a flat bed of mud.
name: Water Filled Cavern

desc:
The dark chute is less than ten feet wide and filled with cold water with
nothing to hold on to. There is an opening in the stone ceiling about fifteen
feet from the surface of the water, but the vertical walls are smooth and
climbing up seems impossible.
name: Watery Chute

desc:
The cold and opaque water chills you to the bones in this sloping chute. The
bottom is covered by a thick layer of mud that is swirled up by the water at
your every movement. To the west is a rock leaning against the wall.
name: Bottom of the Chute

desc:
Cold water fills the dark depression in the underground. The bottom is covered
by a soft layer of mud that is whirled up by the water with every swim stroke
you make. The depression opens into a dome-shaped cavern above. Swimming up into
the cavern seems to be your only option for reaching air soon.
name: Water Filled Depression

desc:
Tiny ripples move across the surface of the dark and putrid water that fills
this hole in the mountain. Black curving stone structures leap smoothly across
the ceiling above the lake. Darkness lurks in the corners as if hiding
something. From the water rises a faint mist that sweeps across the surface
filling every depression in the walls that surrounds the lake.
name: On a Dark Underground Lake

desc:
The misty air in the cavern clings to the walls building a thin layer of dew
that makes the blue rock glisten. A strong stale odour is in the air; combined
with the moisture it leaves a strange taste when inhaled. In some spots the
walls are covered by a layer of fungi and green moss. Care must be taken not to
slip on the wet moss-covered stones that form the floor of the cavern.
name: Moist Cavern

desc:
A vast stone ceiling looms above the lake. Deep unbroken darkness emerges from
dark fissures and deep cracks in the ceiling. Out of the darkness come chilling
sounds echoing between the rock and the surface of the water, making the
presence of bats very apparent. From where the lake meets the rock can be heard
a steady clucking sound as water enters and leaves through hidden cracks in the
rock.
name: On a Dark Underground Lake

desc:
The gloomy waters of this dreary lake have a treacherous look to them. The moist
air is chilly and filled with wisps of fog rising from the surface of the dark
water. High above looms a vast ceiling that catches every sound, casting it back
as a dull echo. The putrid water seems to be moving slowly up and down as if it
was breathing.
name: On a Dark Underground Lake

desc:
Above the dark lake rises a vast stone ceiling concealed in darkness. The edge
of the water is barely discernible in the deep gloom where all details seem to
be interfluent and without any definition. The cold water is slowly rising and
falling, as if something below it was breathing. The surrounding cavern walls
are torn with deep fissures and long cracks, and as the water folds around the
rock in its repetitive movement, tiny ripples are sent across the lake.
name: On a Dark Underground Lake

desc:
Water drips from the ceiling all around you, falling into small puddles on the
ground. At your feet, small rivulets of water flow gently across the cold stone
floor. On the walls grow thick layers of moss wreathing around black moulds. The
damp air is heavy and so hard to breathe that gasping for air seems mandatory.
name: Moist Tunnel

desc:
This passage is worn, but it does not appear to be used at all by the dwarves of
the Blue Mountains. A coating of dust and thick cobwebs covers the floor, and
behind cracks and fissures in the broken rock, eyes of tiny spiders seem to be
staring in your direction.
name: Webbed Passageway

desc:
In the narrow tunnel black walls surround you in a deep darkness. The air is
chokingly dense and fills your mouth with an acidic taste as you breathe. Grey
and black cobwebs span across the tunnel sending loose ends out towards you. The
air greeting you from the south feels even denser.
name: Cobweb Tunnel

desc:
The darkness seems to be alive, as if slowly creeping nearer and nearer, trying
to exhaust hope, quench bravery and instil fear among all who venture into its
dark embrace. There is a feeling in the air of years of suppressed anger ready
to explode in dark rage. Inky shadows in the blackness flit around, peering
coldly into your soul with a paralysing determination. An opening covered with
thick dark threads of spider webs lies to the east, emanating an almost palpable
feeling of misfortune.
name: Entrance of a Dark Lair

desc:
You are in a broad cavern deep in the underground beneath the Blue Mountains.
Between cracked shards aimed at you, the broken floor is littered with bones and
torn fabric in many colours. Across the cavern span sticky webs spun by the dark
minds of the spiders lurking nearby, waiting only for their trap to close on
you. An empowering darkness seems to emanate from the webs.
name: Lair of the Spiders

desc:
In the dark lair the presence of spiders is apparent from the many cobwebs
strung from the ceiling to the floor. The threads are black and if viewed at
certain angles they are nearly invisible as if designed to lure the prey towards
captivity. A fusty smell of rotten flesh and acidulous moisture greets the
beholder. A deep darkness gradually closes in, making any sense of direction
vague and the image of the broken stone walls blurry.
name: Lair of the Spiders

desc:
The rocky cavern walls, covered by layers of spider webs, loom deceptively
around you. The smell in the lair is horrible. A mixture of rotting flesh and
mouldy fabrics torn to pieces litters the floor. Darkness surrounds you with a
fierce determination, bringing fear and despair into every mind. The den seems
to thrive in the darkness, as if the spiders and the darkness were one.
name: Back of the Lair

desc:
Sticky threads of cobwebs dominate the view. From every conceivable outcrop in
the walls and ceiling they grab hold and span across the lair in a fine black
mesh. Some of the threads are hanging loose from the ceiling and they look alive
as they swing back and forth in an attempt to grab hold of anything trappable.
An impenetrable darkness lurking in the cavern blurs the view of the cavern
walls and any sense of direction.
name: Lair of the Spiders

desc:
An obnoxious stench of rotten flesh hurtles towards you. Obscured by dense
cobwebs, the darkened walls of the lair fade out of sight in the intrusive
darkness that fills every corner and crack in the cavern. Mighty webs span out
across the lair with fine threads searching for a victim to ensnare. The floor
is slippery, covered by many layers of dirt.
name: Lair of the Spiders

desc:
This tunnel was once part of the vast mines beneath the Blue Mountains, but a
recent cave-in turned it into a very narrow crawlspace. Crawling on hands and
knees one could just barely manoeuvre through the rubble blocking the way. While
cramped in this tight space, the sounds of nearby mining seem amplified.
name: Collapsed Mine Tunnel

desc:
Although the old mine is finely crafted, the utilitarian nature of this mining
area is obvious in the unadorned tunnel walls and the heavy flags of the stone
floor. Days in which the old mine saw the energetic horde of dwarven miners
burrowing their way though the mountain are long gone. Only faint sounds of
mining activity elsewhere break the quiet decrepitude of the old mine.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The floor here is quite level and paved with huge stone flags. It would appear
that it was crafted this way to support heavy loads passing through. The sound
of dripping water fills the air; looking closer at the rocky walls one can see
tiny streams of water trickle down from the ceiling and disappear through near
invisible cracks in the floor. Though the mining of these caves has long ceased,
signs of the work that once filled these mines are clearly visible in the rugged
walls.
name: Old Mine

desc:
Judging by the rifts and cracks in the barren stone walls, this old mine once
played the part of a productive ore source in the Dwarven excavation of the Blue
Mountains. Now the ore has been extracted and the old mine has been partially
abandoned. Only occasionally a few dwarves might pass through the mine on their
way to the more productive areas within.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The rugged stone walls lean inwards to a crumbling ceiling. There is no precise
definition to either walls or ceiling and where they conjoin there are only
loose rocks just waiting to fall apart. Measures have been taken to support the
loose rocks by a wooden skeleton. Presumably the tunnel was created with the
intention of providing easy access between various parts of the dwarven mines.
name: Worn Mine Tunnel

desc:
The barren ceiling is supported by a wooden structure of strong beams that joins
with planks along the walls. The need for support is highly justified by the
loose rocks sprayed across the floor underneath a nearby cave-in in a less
supported area of the ceiling. The rumbling sound of metal mining its way
through hard rock echoes through the dark-walled tunnel.
name: Worn Mine Tunnel

desc:
Relentless digging performed by the dwarves in the past seems to be the cause of
the worn appearance of this tunnel. Torn with dwarven determination, the rough
tunnel had been emptied of ore long ago, and now not even the most skilled miner
would stand a chance of finding ore here. A faint blue colour in the stone is
the last remnant of the richness once held by the mountain.
name: Worn Tunnel

desc:
Deep cracks, rendered into the hard rock by the efficient mining of the dwarves,
lie in the shadows in the surrounding walls. Proficient strikes of many pickaxes
carved and mined this tunnel in the past. Now the mine is empty but for a few
spiders scurrying in the corners and building their tiny webs in hope of
trapping anything edible.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The tunnel proceeds through a region where the predominant blue rock slowly
turns grey and dull. The rock is fairly loose here, and oak planks have been
placed as reinforcement where the need for support seems inevitable to keep the
tunnel from caving in. In the distance is heard the wailing sound of the
mountain giving way to the children of Aule.
name: Worn Mine Tunnel

desc:
Carved out of bluish rock, this tunnel stands as proof of the magnificent skills
that are commonly found among the dwarven mine-workers. Even though this part of
the underground is clearly emptied of all valuable minerals, it is still used
for transporting minerals from parts where mining is still taking place. From
somewhere east the sound of dwarves talking, wheels turning and pickaxes digging
their way through the earth can be heard.
name: Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel bears marks of heavy mining. In the rough walls are deep holes dug by
the dwarves as they went forward in their search for ore. In other places, where
the success of finding ore seemed less apparent, hardly a single strike had been
performed. This form of selective mining seems to be paying off, as the sound of
dwarves cheering in the distance can be heard whenever a new lode of ore has
been struck.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
A quick glance shows that this part of the tunnels has been long empty of ore
and is now used only for transportation of ore from other regions within. From
time to time a dwarf might pass by, singing a merry tune of wealth and fortune,
but besides that and the faint sound of working miners coming from somewhere to
the north, the tunnel is silent.
name: Old Mine

desc:
This mine once employed a great host of dwarven mine-workers that dug through
the mountain in search of ore. Now the empty walls stand as silent testimony to
the intensive mining that once filled the tunnels with the sounds of metal
hitting the stone.
name: Old Mine

desc:
The solid rock in which these mines are excavated carries the echoes of every
sound you make through the tunnels. But from the look of the tunnel it doesn't
seem likely that anybody is here to hear them. This part has been emptied a long
time ago and is now just a proof of the dwarves' eager search for wealth. The
air here is fresh and clean, but some moisture can be detected.
name: Old Mine Tunnel

desc:
Surrounded by sharp teeth-shaped rocks, a twisting passage leads through the
dark underground. Little space is available to move between the narrowing walls
where any attempt to walk would soon end in crawling. A veil of dust fills every
nook and cranny. The sound of nearby mining echoes through the passage.
name: Twisting Passage

desc:
A multitude of different fungi thrive in the moist conditions on the walls of
the passage. Condensed water runs off the walls and gathers in small pools on
the floor. As the ceiling dives low over a larger pool, one would need to crawl
to pass through and it would be hard to avoid getting ones feet wet.
name: Watery Passage

desc:
The fissure is filled with soaking wet fungi that seem to thrive in the moist
conditions. Barely avoiding a pointy protrusion in the western wall one can
avoid the fungi and proceed through the fissure with nearly dry feet. Examining
the roof closer, you notice a series of very narrow cracks that disappear in the
rock as deep as any light would go.
name: Dark and Watery Fissure

desc:
Arching across the ceiling vast stone constructions run in parallel stretches
down towards the floor where they conjoin with deep, torn crevices. The floor,
broken by evanescent cracks, blurs lightly with a deep blue colour. The cavern
is natural, and there are no visible signs of Dwarven presence.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
The crumbling fissure with grinding spikes of hard rock writhes in its
tortuosity trying to elude the darkness that lurks in every corner. The
decrepitude of the broken surroundings makes poor living conditions even for the
minor life forms, yet the sensation of something lurking in the darkness is
apparent. The scrutiny of unseen eyes is upon you.
name: A Dark, Twisting Fissure

desc:
A dark cleft inside the mountain has been partially filled with cold water.
Loose rocks and boulders have fallen into the water and are now only visible as
skerries just below the water surface. Deeply cut scars in the cold stone walls
provide adequate grip for climbing through the fissure without falling into the
water.
name: Watery Fissure

desc:
Rocks, boulders, cracks and holes would make travelling through the twisting
passage a hard work. The passage often widens, only to narrow again, or to
contract into a narrow crawlspace behind the next corner. The air is moist and
tiny beads of water gently trickle down the walls drawing lines in the dust.
From the north can be heard the unmistakable sound of mining.
name: Twisting Passage

desc:
Curving obediently with the ceiling, blue stone veins follow a twisting passage
through the underground. Fierce spikes of rock, rooted in the ceiling, wield
sharp edges in objection to any trespasser. At times the path passes by open
fissures from which only calm darkness sends its greetings. Stale, dusty air
makes breathing difficult.
name: Twisting Passage

desc:
Confined by cracked walls of hard rock, the passage winds onward. Every movement
is a test of agility as the twisting passage curls around sharp protrusions of
rocks. As the passage turns yet another sharp corner, it suddenly begins to
descend a perilous slope. The sound of mining echoes through the passage.
name: Twisting Passage

desc:
Each step forward in the narrow confinement of the hostile surroundings would be
a test of dexterity. Sharp rocks point their edges towards anything passing
through, aiming to scratch and tear clothes and gear. The sound of mining can be
heard as a distant war of rock and metal.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
A narrow opening in the underground leads past a large chasm and a ledge under a
low ceiling of spiked rocks allows safe access past it. Darkness prevails in the
chasm; nothing seems to escape the deep except the unmistakable sounds of bats.
The passage continues its twisting route beyond the ledge, winding smoothly
around corners and outcrops in the rock.
name: Twisting Passage

desc:
Filling the surroundings in the cavern is a deep darkness that lurks in cracks
and fissures in the grim solitude of the barren walls. The floor is torn with
deep crevices and there is little distinction between it and the walls; the
floor is merely an incline that leads upwards heading for the ceiling. The
cavern is natural, and no signs of dwarven work can be seen in it.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
A large dome-shaped cavern wrapped in shadows extends its arching ceiling over a
series of undisturbed pools in the floor. With some care, passage is made
possible by slippery gangways leading between the fungi-filled shores of the
pools. The dark water in the pools exudes a musty and uncomfortable smell.
name: Wet Cavern

desc:
Wedged between the splintered walls and the descending roof, the passage forces
its way through the core of the Blue Mountains. Shards of rock protrude from the
floor everywhere except for a small opening beneath a gaping hole in the ceiling
caused by a recent cave-in. An enveloping darkness lurks here, as if waiting to
quench any source of light.
name: Broken Passage

desc:
Beneath a curving ceiling a narrow passage snakes through stone formations deep
under the Blue Mountains. Deep fissures cross the walls and the floor, and care
must be taken as the narrowing passage winds past the deep chasms of darkness. A
faint trace of moisture fills the air over one of the larger pits. Travel here
is tiring and little rest can to be found on the rough stone.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
The passage twists through the underground of black rock with gaping fissures.
Little space is available for manoeuvring through the tight space confined by
the barren protrusions of curving stone walls. Great care must be taken to avoid
bumping into the outmost of the rocks that obscure the passage.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
The tunnel shows no signs of ever being mined actively. But being known for
their stubbornness, the dwarves dug their way through this area anyway in hope
of finding iron minerals further on. The building, however, must have been
difficult as the rock is fairly loose in some areas, and the ceiling in the
tunnel is supported in several locations by wooden planks to prevent a cave-in.
name: Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
Beneath the Dwarven Homes lies the vast complex of the dwarven mines. As the
mine gradually expanded towards the discovery of new ore deposits, empty and
dusty tunnels were formed on the way. The dark walls of the tunnel tell the
story of the extensive labour that has enabled the dwarves to extract ore from
the deepest part of the mountain, as pickaxes swung with great strength and
determination against the rock left deep scars in the stone.
name: Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
Apparently unhindered, the tunnel advances through the mountain. The tunnel
looks as if it was excavated recently and is just beginning to take shape. A low
ceiling, cracked and torn with deep marks, is making its way further and deeper
into the dark underground. A distant sound of metal striking hard rock indicates
that work is going on elsewhere in the mine.
name: Dwarven Tunnel

desc:
The walls of the tunnel seem to have been crafted recently. The hard rock that
was once the solid foundation of the mountain has been crushed into fragments by
the busy workmen who formed the tunnel. Precise and efficient were the movements
that swung the tools searching for ore. The cracked walls leave little doubt
about the richness of the mountain, as any light reflected by the rock is cast
back in a strange deep blue colour.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
Here in the dwarven mines deep inside the Blue Mountains the tremendous work of
the dwarves becomes apparent indeed. Through great labour the dwarves have dug
through the deep roots of the mountain in search of ore. This mine is proof of
rich deposits of iron. Trapped just beneath the surface of the rock a thick lode
of iron emits an evanescent metallic blur. On the rubble-strewn floor rest
several empty buckets used to transport ore.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
The tunnel passes through the heart of the dwarven mine. A big lode of ore must
be nearby, as the mountain echoes with the sounds of dwarves working, driven by
the will to make new discoveries. Discarded mining tools can be seen on the
ground; apparently, the tools gave up long before their owners.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
Scars and chips bulge out from the sides of the tunnel, showing obvious signs of
heavy mining over the long years. Now the tunnel stands empty and barren, and
judging by its abandoned look, one should not expect to find here much ore.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
Through cracks and scars in the walls the purpose of this mine becomes clear.
There is ore here. Glinting in the dark with a pale metallic light, the precious
metal awaits the tools that will set it free. The mine echoes with the
repetitive sound of axes hitting at the stone and the wails of the mountain
reluctant to give up its treasures.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
The presence of ore is very apparent here. Long lines in the walls gleam with
the omnipresent metallic shine. An echoing sound of metal thrust against hard
rock rolls through the mine as the ore is mined by skilful hands. Rock bound
together for ages by the weight of the mountain is released with each clanging
sound of tools working their way through. Busy are the dwarves and in their
labour they thrive.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
You are in the eastern part of the dwarven mines bearing closer to the east side
of the mountain. The rumbling sound of rock giving way to the dwarven pickaxes
echoes through the mine. Into the eastern wall a tunnel passes under an arch of
coal-black rock, widening slightly ahead.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
A narrow passage winds its way through the mountain here. Bending a little, one
can just barely squeeze through it. The walls are cold and covered with a dew
that makes the surface of the rock shine with a pale silvery glow. The sound of
metal eating its way through the foundation of the Blue Mountains can be heard
from the north.
name: Dark Side Passage

desc:
A narrow ceiling soars upwards carried by strong beams of oak reaching from wall
to wall. The wood seems more decorative than functional as the sturdy rock could
easily support itself. The walls, straight and tall, mauled from the rock of the
mountain, rise high reaching to conjoin with the ceiling, more than at the
height of three men from the ground. In the northern wall there is a large
niche.
name: Narrow Hall

desc:
The chamber, cut into the hard mountain rock, shows signs of recent digging in
an attempt to enlarge it. The work appears to have been finished recently and
the chamber now acts as a storage space. Broad wooden shelves mounted on the
surrounding walls serve as a holding area for food and other goods.
name: Storage Chamber

desc:
The cavern winds its smoothly curving walls around a stairway leading upwards in
the centre. A coal-black rock floor serves as a steadfast foundation for the
ascending stairway. Near the roof of the cavern is a wooden platform stretched
from wall to wall. The ringing sound of metal forcing its strength upon hard
rock can be heard in the distance.
name: Circular Cavern

desc:
Strong oak planks span across a cavern some fifteen feet below. The platform
easily carries more than a dozen men in full armour, yet it seems that there is
more to this construction than meets the eye. The roof of grey rock, chiselled
with the utmost care, casts dull shadows on the platform. In the centre of the
platform is a rectangular hole, large enough for three dwarves to descend at
once.
name: Wooden Platform

desc:
Fissures and cracks in the walls lie shaded in a dense vapour. Faint lines of
water trickle down from the ceiling bending around outcrops and disappearing
into dark funnels. The amount of moisture in the air is greatest near the
southern wall where a deep dark hole seems to spout dense clouds towards you.
The floor is slippery and care must be taken not to trip.
name: Moist Cavern

desc:
Dripping with moisture, the broken walls cringe up towards a dark ceiling that
seems to envelop the damp air in the tunnel. Bulbous fungi thrive on the moist
rock. A foul stench comes from the exposed surface of black moulds that battle
for space with a variety of other fungi species. The dense and vaporous air
causes shallow pools of water to form as it condenses on the cold stone floor.
name: Moist Tunnel

desc:
The dark cavern ceiling curves slightly above, supported by strong pillars
covered by a slimy green substance. A musty smell pervades the moist air, mixed
with a strong odour of sulphur. The cavern floor has a gleaming aura of light
reflected from myriad tiny water-beads. Numerous fungi and other slimy creatures
thrive in the moist conditions. There are no apparent signs that dwarves ever
visited this place.
name: Moist Cavern
